matter.title: Grace C. Roe v. Maria Q. Fairbanks (eviction)
matter.summary: Grace C. Roe seeks to evict Maria Q. Fairbanks from 22 Tidewater Lane, Apt 3, Augusta based on termination of a tenancy at will, having served a 7-day notice to quit for nonpayment of rent on 2026-02-26.

fact_pattern.summary: Grace C. Roe seeks to evict Maria Q. Fairbanks from 22 Tidewater Lane, Apt 3, Augusta based on termination of a tenancy at will, having served a 7-day notice to quit for nonpayment of rent on 2026-02-26.
fact_pattern.narrative:
Grace C. Roe rents the residential unit at 22 Tidewater Lane, Apt 3 in Augusta, Maine to Maria Q. Fairbanks under a month-to-month tenancy at will at $2097 per month. The tenant is approximately 6 month(s) behind, owing about $3911.

The landlord served a 7-day notice to quit for nonpayment of rent on 2026-02-26. The notice period has expired and the tenant remains in possession, so the landlord now brings a Forcible Entry and Detainer action seeking a judgment for possession (and, where allowed, unpaid rent).

The case proceeds as summary process in the District Court. The tenant may raise defenses such as breach of the implied warranty of habitability or improper notice, which the court must resolve before issuing a writ of possession.
undisputed_facts: Maria Q. Fairbanks occupies the unit at 22 Tidewater Lane, Apt 3, Augusta.
A 7-day notice to quit for nonpayment of rent was served on 2026-02-26.
disputed_facts: The exact amount of rent owed and any setoff for conditions.
Whether the premises met the implied warranty of habitability.
Whether the notice was proper in form and timing.

third_parties.name: Lighthouse Outfitters Corp.
third_parties.relevance: Manages the building and documented the nonpayment.

third_parties.name: Marcus H. Moe
third_parties.relevance: Serves the summons and any writ of possession.

third_parties.name: Regional housing authority
third_parties.relevance: Administers the tenant's housing voucher, if any.

third_parties.name: Carmen F. Fairbanks
third_parties.relevance: Can testify to disturbances or occupancy.

exchanges.question: What is the rental arrangement?
exchanges.answer: a month-to-month tenancy at will at $2097 a month for the unit at 22 Tidewater Lane, Apt 3.

exchanges.question: What is the problem?
exchanges.answer: The tenant is about 6 months behind, owing roughly $3911. The basis is termination of a tenancy at will.

exchanges.question: What notice did you give?
exchanges.answer: I served a 7-day notice to quit for nonpayment of rent on 2026-02-26, and the period has run.

exchanges.question: Has the tenant complained about conditions?
exchanges.answer: There may be a habitability argument; I want to be ready for it.

exchanges.question: What do you want?
exchanges.answer: Possession of the unit, and the unpaid rent if the court will award it.

goals: Obtain a judgment for possession of the premises.
Recover unpaid rent where the court allows it in the FED action.
Secure a writ of possession if the tenant does not vacate.
priorities: Proper notice and service to avoid dismissal.
desired_outcomes: A judgment for possession and issuance of a writ if needed.
non_starters: A defective notice that forces the case to be refiled.

constraints.budget: Cost-conscious; the landlord wants a prompt, clean summary process.
constraints.timeline: Wants possession before the unit's carrying costs mount further.

issues.issue: Implied warranty of habitability as a defense
Grounds for forcible entry and detainer
sub_questions: Is there a valid statutory ground for eviction?
Is there any claim of retaliation?

issues.issue: Issuance and execution of the writ of possession
sub_questions: When may the writ issue after judgment?

evidence.title: Notice to quit with proof of service
evidence.description: The served notice that starts the clock.

amounts.label: Estimated unit carrying cost per month

motions.title: Request for issuance of writ of possession
motions.relief: A writ directing the tenant's removal if the judgment is not satisfied.

affirmative_defenses.basis: The notice to quit did not comply with 14 M.R.S. § 6002.
Eviction allegedly in response to a complaint. 14 M.R.S. § 6001.

facts.notice_type: 7-day notice to quit for nonpayment of rent
facts.eviction_basis: termination of a tenancy at will
